template_name: Standard Prescription Format – Bathing a Newborn

first_bath_recommendation.term_stable_newborn: After 24 hours of life (or at least after 6 hours if necessary)
first_bath_recommendation.sick_or_unstable_baby: Wait until baby is stable and thermoregulated

bathing_type_advice.description: Wipe with wet cloth, no immersion
bathing_type_advice.use_when: Before cord falls off or in preterm babies
Once cord has fallen off and baby is stable

bathing_type_advice.description: Clean face, hands, neck folds, diaper area only
bathing_type_advice.use_when: On non-bath days or in winter

materials_needed: Mild fragrance-free baby cleanser if needed

standard_instructions.room_temperature: Keep room warm (at least 25°C) and free from drafts
method: Undress baby gradually and keep wrapped as much as possible
Clean eyes from inner to outer side using separate clean cotton
Clean face, neck, and diaper area gently
Support baby's head and neck throughout bath
Use gentle strokes and avoid vigorous scrubbing
Pat dry thoroughly, especially skin folds
Feed after bath if needed and monitor warmth

what_to_avoid: Bathing before 24 hours of life unless necessary
Using harsh soaps, antiseptics, or strong cleansers
Bathing in cold or drafty rooms
Use of talcum powder after bath

optional_skin_care.emollient: May apply gentle emollient after bath if skin is dry
optional_skin_care.soap_use: Soap or cleanser is optional; if used, choose mild fragrance-free product

counselling: Delay the first bath; vernix has protective benefits — पहला नहाना देर से करें; वर्निक्स (सफेद परत) बच्चे की त्वचा की रक्षा करती है
Sponge bath is enough until cord falls off — नाल गिरने तक स्पंज से पोंछना काफी है
Avoid over-bathing; 2–3 baths per week are sufficient — ज़्यादा नहलाने से बचें; हफ्ते में 2–3 बार काफी है
Daily cleaning of face, neck folds, and diaper area is more important than frequent full baths — चेहरे, गर्दन की सिलवटों और डायपर वाली जगह की रोज़ सफ़ाई, बार-बार नहलाने से ज़्यादा ज़रूरी है
Always support the baby's head and neck during bath — नहलाते समय बच्चे के सिर और गर्दन को हमेशा सहारा दें
Keep baby warm before, during, and after the bath — नहलाने से पहले, बीच में और बाद में बच्चे को गर्म रखें
Seek medical advice if baby becomes cold, blue, excessively sleepy, or develops skin irritation — अगर बच्चा ठंडा, नीला, ज़्यादा सुस्त हो जाए या त्वचा पर जलन हो तो डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
warning_signs: Baby turns blue or pale during or after bath — नहाते समय या बाद में बच्चा नीला या पीला पड़ जाए
Baby feels cold to touch after bath — नहाने के बाद बच्चा छूने में ठंडा लगे
Excessive crying that does not settle — बहुत ज़्यादा रोना जो शांत न हो
Skin rash, redness or peeling after bath — नहाने के बाद त्वचा पर दाने, लालिमा या छिलना
Vomiting or drowsiness after bath — नहाने के बाद उल्टी या सुस्ती
Cord area becomes red, smelly or has pus — नाल वाली जगह लाल हो जाए, बदबू आए या मवाद निकले
expected_course: Newborns generally tolerate bathing well once stable and thermoregulated. The first bath can be safely given after 24 hours of life. Sponge baths are recommended until the umbilical cord falls off (usually 7–14 days). Skin dryness and mild peeling in the first 2 weeks is normal and resolves spontaneously.
key_clinical_points: WHO recommends delaying the first bath to at least 24 hours after birth
Vernix caseosa is protective — do not scrub it off
Hypothermia risk is highest in preterm and LBW babies during bathing
Room temperature should be at least 25°C during bath
Water temperature 37°C — test with elbow or thermometer
Sponge bath until umbilical cord separates
No soap needed for neonates; plain warm water is sufficient

follow_up_advice.routine_follow_up: At next newborn or well-baby visit
